rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Ritual / practice (as noun) / ceremony — hard ban.
No stale markers, no 'fixed' retrospectives, no session biography.
Register match — Bootstrap voice trio (Godin × Sutherland × Siilasmaa).
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 5 (Sutherland reframe): 'A chat forgets. A memory remembers.' Line 107-109 (Sutherland + Siilasmaa): 'A library without a librarian is a cost. Give it one.' Peer-warm Godin tone throughout ('Don't. Three beats'; direct second person).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't smuggle terms of art from maintainer into body.
Value-prop / positioning belongs in sales copy, NOT student-facing body.
Watch out for planting jargon too early.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 5 earns 'memory' via direct contrast with 'chat'. Line 108-109 earns 'agent' with one-breath primer: 'is a markdown file: instructions the model reads at the start of every run.' No premature LLM-internal vocabulary.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Aspirational destination, never corrective gap. No contemplative-pause theatre or room-share commands.
Earn every technical term on first use.
rules_evaluated.evidence: 'Connector' earned line 28 in list context (wiki, docs, storage, chat, email). 'Agent' earned Phase 2 with structural definition.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No narrating the student's behavior, state, or accomplishments.
Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Beat 1 (line 21): 'Claude surveys what's available and asks about your world.' Beat 2 (line 40): 'Now Claude pulls the content into sources/. Agent does the heavy lifting.' Beat 3 (line 73): 'Now the memory gets built from what's actually on disk.' Self-audit (line 92): 'Now ask Claude to audit itself.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Canonical presentation shape — lead-in, label, blank line, fenced block.
rules_evaluated.evidence: All four prompt blocks follow canonical shape: semantic lead-in, '**Prompt** *(Claude Code)*', blank line, fenced block. No 'copy →' prefix, no '(end of prompt)' closer.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Runtime-fork prompt blocks: paired divs, shared chip.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 42, 60: span-level runtime forks for mechanical content (CLI vs Desktop vs Cowork path differences). Lines 62-71: div-level paired forks for Beat 3 plan-mode explanation (rt-code vs rt-cowork). Both patterns preserve shared canonical prompt chip.

Bootstrap Module 2 mood is discovery and agency. Exercise honors this throughout: ownership language ('you build a memory', 'your first custom agent'), autonomy through phases, agent design step where student writes rules. No mood flattening.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with the discipline, not the failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Title leads with 'memory' (positive discipline). Openings lead with capability: 'A memory is only as good as what goes into it' (curation discipline). Phase 2 opens with 'A library without a librarian is a cost. Give it one.' (agent-as-discipline). No failure-mode framing.
